Search_NoResultsDescription: Try another keyword, or clear search to see every tool in the current category.
Engine_MockDescription: This is a local mock status for validating the client shell. It will connect to the Rust desktop engine later.
Tool_ToolboxHome_Description: Return to the Fowan tool launcher.
Tool_QuickCapture_Description: Capture text, clipboard notes, and small ideas without choosing a final destination.
Tool_Todo_Description: Open the standalone Todo tool for tasks and sticky mode.
Tool_Diary_Description: Open the standalone Diary tool for daily writing and timeline review.
Tool_Knowledge_Description: Collect durable knowledge items and sources.
Tool_GlobalSearch_Description: Search across tools, files, and knowledge.
Tool_AI_Description: Ask for assistance with selected context.
Tool_AIChat_Description: Use configured credentials and models for streaming conversations.
Tool_AIConfig_Description: Manage AI providers, credentials, models, and tool defaults.
Tool_Plugins_Description: Manage plugins, MCP servers, and skills.
Tool_Settings_Description: Adjust theme, language, startup, and privacy basics.
Tool_Diagnostics_Description: Inspect client, protocol, engine status, and recent errors.
Tool_LaunchMissing: Could not find {0}. Build the tool first.
Tool_PlannedReason: This tool is planned for a later release.
QuickCapture_Saved: Quick Capture saved to the local session.
Settings_AutoStart: Start Fowan when I sign in
Settings_AutoStart_Description: Start Fowan silently in the notification area when you sign in to Windows.
Settings_UpdateCheck_Description: Check GitHub Releases when Fowan starts and prompt when a newer stable version is available.
Update_Description: You are running Fowan {0}. Version {1} can be installed now.
Update_DisableCheck: Do not check for updates automatically
Empty_NoTools: No tools in this category yet.
AI_SecretStoreNotice: Keys are secured by Windows Credential Manager
AI_LocalOnly: Keys and history stay on this device
AI_ChatWelcomeDescription: Choose a credential and model, then enter a message. History stays encrypted on this device.
AI_ConfigurationRequired: Finish AI setup to start chatting
AI_AddCredentialGuide: No enabled credential is configured. Add a provider credential in AI Configuration.
AI_AddModelGuide: The current credential has no enabled model. Add a model in AI Configuration.
AI_NoCredentialsDescription: Choose a built-in provider or add a custom endpoint, then store its API key in Windows Credential Manager.
AI_NoModelsDescription: After adding a credential, choose a preset model or enter a custom model ID.
AI_InputPlaceholder: Type a message, use / to choose a skill, Shift + Enter for a new line
AI_ReplaceApiKey: Replace API Key (leave blank to keep it)
AI_DefaultChatDescription: New AI chats use this credential and model by default. You can still switch inside a chat.
AI_BaseUrlDefault: Leave blank to use the channel default
AI_SelectConfiguration: Select an available credential and model first.
AI_CredentialTestRequiresModel: Add and enable a model for this credential before testing the connection.
AI_SelectConversation: Open a chat with a user message and select a credential and model first.
AI_Error_Auth: Authentication failed. Check the API key and endpoint.
AI_Error_Model: The model does not exist or is unavailable to this credential.
AI_Error_RateLimit: The provider rate or quota limit was reached. Try again later.
AI_Error_Context: This chat exceeds the model context limit. Start a new chat.
AI_Error_Timeout: The request timed out. Check the network and try again.
AI_Error_Conflict: This configuration is still referenced and cannot be deleted.
AI_Error_SecretStore: Windows secure credential storage is unavailable.
AI_Error_Unavailable: AI is unavailable. Check Core, network, and model configuration.
AI_PresetOptional: Optional; you can also enter a model ID
